message:
fix(search): Correct arithmetic order in cache shard cost tracking

The LRU cache cost tracking had a subtle bug in the order of operations.
Previously, the code would:
1. Read the old entry's cost via get()
2. Insert the new entry via put()
3. Compute: total += (new - old)

The issue: lru::LruCache::put() returns the evicted value, but get()
was called before put(), so we'd look up the key, then put() would
return None (since get() doesn't remove) - missing the replaced value.

Fix the arithmetic by:
1. Call put() first and capture its return value (the replaced entry)
2. Compute old_cost/old_bytes from the replaced value
3. Use saturating_add(new).saturating_sub(old) instead of
   saturating_sub(old_cost) to handle cases where old > new without
   underflow

This ensures cache byte accounting remains consistent, particularly
important for the memory-bounded cache eviction logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
